zh.Thornton, Cheshire, England: 英國英格蘭爾桑頓 馬福
zh.Thornton-le-Moors, Cheshire, England: 英國英格蘭爾桑頓 勒 波斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda Thornton於大約1325年出生在英國英格蘭爾桑頓 馬福時，她的父親Peter Thorton40歲，母親Lucia Hellesby31歲。她於大約1340年與Henry de Beeston在英國英格蘭爾貝斯頓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1365年在英國爾貝斯頓享年41歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Matilda Thornton于大约1325年出生在英国英格兰爾桑頓 馬福时，她的父亲Peter Thorton40岁，母亲Lucia Hellesby31岁。她于大约1340年与Henry de Beeston在英国英格兰爾貝斯頓结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世于1365年在英国爾貝斯頓享年41岁。

nameMeanings.name: Thornton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of numerous places throughout England and Scotland so called, from Old English thorn ‘thorn tree’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The placename is most frequent in northern England, especially Yorkshire, where there are at least 16 possible sources for the surname.
Irish: Anglicized (translated) form of Gaelic Mac Sceacháin ‘son of Sceachán’ (see Skehan ).
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Draighneáin ‘descendant of Draighneán’ (see Drennan ).

variants.name: Thornber
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the five minor places in Yorkshire called Thornber, particularly Thornber in Halton West. The placenames derive from Old English thorn ‘thorn’ + beorg ‘hill, mound’.
Irish: adopted for various Irish names containing an element meaning ‘thorn’; compare   Thornton  .

variants.name: Thorton
meanings: English: probably a shortened form of   Thornton  , or a habitational name from Thorton in Marnhull, Dorset, which has the same origin.

variants.name: Skehan
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Sceacháin or Ó Sceacháin ‘son (or descendant) of Sceachán’, a personal name probably based on a diminutive of sceach ‘briar, thorn bush’. The Gaelic name has sometimes been ‘translated’ as Thornton .

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda Thornton was born about 1325, in Thornton Mayow, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Peter Thorton, was 40 and her mother, Lucia Hellesby, was 31. She married Henry de Beeston about 1340, in Beeston, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died in 1365, in Beeston, Cheshire, England, at the age of 41.

Elle a épousé Henry de Beeston environ 1340, à Beeston, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée en 1365, à Beeston, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 41 ans.

ko.Thornton-le-Moors, Cheshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 토른턴 일 무르스
ko.lifeSummary: Matilda Thornton(이)가 대략 1325년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 토른턴 메이요에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Peter Thorton(은)는 40세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lucia Hellesby(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 대략 1340년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 브이스턴에서 Henry de Beeston(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1365년 영국 체실르 브이스턴에서 41세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Thornton-le-Moors, Cheshire, England: таурнтон л мурс, чешир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matilda Thornton родилась около 1325 г. в местности таурнтон меиоу, чешир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Peter Thorton было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Lucia Hellesby, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry de Beeston около 1340 г. в местности бистон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла в 1365 г. в местности бистон, чешир, Англия в возрасте 41 год.
